steps.title: Identify the structural boundary
steps.content:
The sentence contains two independent clauses: one about when van Gogh completed the artwork (It was April of 1885 when Vincent van Gogh completed [title]), and a second about the drawing's significance (The drawing is an important part of Van Gogh's body of work).

The second clause needs proper sentence-boundary punctuation to separate it from the first.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content:
Option B correctly places a period after the title, starting a fresh sentence: The drawing, one of dozens to be featured in the upcoming exhibition, is an important part of Van Gogh's body of work. The subject “The drawing” is properly followed by an appositive phrase in commas, then the verb “is”—a grammatically correct structure.

Option A violates the sentence boundary rule by joining two independent clauses with only a comma, while options C and D place an incorrect comma between the subject and main verb.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
